danger_signals.visual_form: boss character sprites with distinct color palettes and larger frames than mooks
danger_signals.meaning: encounter imminent threat; escalating danger tier

danger_signals.visual_form: multiple generic enemy sprites on screen simultaneously
coordinated enemy waves with uniform appearance
danger_signals.meaning: centralized authoritarian threat; world conquest plot

danger_signals.visual_form: character sprite damage states or health bar reduction

safety_signals.visual_form: Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, or Namor sprites entering screen
safety_signals.meaning: temporary combat assistance; faction support confirmation

safety_signals.visual_form: four distinct Avenger portraits (Captain America, Iron Man, Hawkeye, Vision)
safety_signals.meaning: player agency and empowerment; faction alignment choice

safety_signals.visual_form: background parallax shift to sky/air environment; flight-capable character activation
safety_signals.meaning: tactical advantage shift; environmental control regain

faction_indicators.color_identity: varied per character (Captain America: red/blue/white; Iron Man: gold/red; Hawkeye: purple; Vision: red/yellow)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: urban and fortified locations as player spaces; defense postures

faction_indicators.color_identity: muted grays, blacks, dark reds; skull iconography
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Red Skull branding; henchmen wear uniform appearance
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: generic mooks visually distinct from named supervillains (Klaw, Living Laser, Whirlwind, Sentinel, Wizard, Grim Reaper, Mandarin, Juggernaut, Ultron, Controller, Crossbones)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: fortress/industrial spaces; enemy strongholds; visual clutter suggesting villainous occupation

faction_indicators.color_identity: distinct per villain (e.g., Sentinel: metallic silver; Ultron: metallic red/gold)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: individual costume/mecha design serves as faction subcategory marker
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: boss sprite larger, more detailed, more animated than generic enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: boss arenas with thematic environmental hazards matching villain identity

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Red Skull's plot active / world under threat
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: enemy presence throughout levels; fortified enemy strongholds; henchmen patrols
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemies advance toward player; coordinated attack patterns; boss encounters trigger escalation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: four playable Avengers available; side-scrolling stage progression; level-by-level villain elimination
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player movement right/left through levels; incremental territory control; boss defeats progress narrative

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, Namor appear as sprite overlays
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: allied characters attack enemies alongside player; temporary combat power increase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: background shifts to sky; Iron Man/Vision fly natively; Captain America/Hawkeye board flying machines
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player gains vertical movement freedom; enemy density changes; shmup-style shooting phase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: arena isolation; boss sprite dominates screen; health bar or damage indicator visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: boss attacks with distinct move patterns; player restricted to arena; defeat is story milestone

environmental_storytelling.example: henchmen clusters in fortified areas suggest Red Skull's operational control; organized formations imply military hierarchy

environmental_storytelling.technique: boss character placement in narrative sequence
environmental_storytelling.example: progression through Klaw → Living Laser → Whirlwind → Sentinel → Wizard → Grim Reaper → Mandarin → Juggernaut → Ultron → Controller → Crossbones → Red Skull creates escalating threat hierarchy and world conquest timeline
each Avenger's visual kit (shield, arrows, armor, vision beam) communicates their combat role and faction allegiance
fortress architecture, industrial settings, and thematic boss arenas suggest Red Skull's technological militarization and territorial occupation
Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, Namor appear as story-driven reinforcements, reinforcing Avengers as unified faction against centralized threat

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: advance toward player; basic attack patterns; eliminated quickly; respawn in waves
distinct attack patterns; larger health pool; arena-bound; defeat triggers story progression
melee attacks; shield throw (ranged special); flying kick; boarding flying machine
melee attacks; energy beam projectiles; native flight; 45-degree angle beam during jump
melee attacks; arrow projectiles; flying kick; boarding flying machine
melee attacks; energy beam projectiles; native flight; 45-degree angle beam during jump

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: allied Avenger (Wasp, Quicksilver, Wonder Man, Namor)
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: auto-attack enemies; provide temporary combat boost; visual confirmation of faction unity
escalated attack complexity; arena mastery; represents culmination of world conquest plot; player victory = narrative resolution

notes: Captain America and the Avengers uses classic beat 'em up visual language to communicate faction identity (Avengers via character costume and insignia vs. Red Skull's army via uniform henchmen appearance and villain distinctiveness). World state is conveyed through enemy density, boss encounter structure (representing threat escalation), and the strategic availability of flight sequences and allied support. The game's narrative (world conquest plot) is manifested through organized enemy strongholds and hierarchical villain encounters. Limited source documentation constrains granular visual detail extraction; visual manifestation inferences derive from beat 'em up genre conventions and gameplay description.
